feat: decompressFrame compressed content — single compressed-block frame output (#1027)

* feat: decompressFrame compressed content — single compressed-block frame output

Add two frame-level content theorems for compressed blocks:
- decompressFrame_single_compressed_literals_content: when a frame contains
  a single last compressed block with numSeq=0, output equals literals
- decompressFrame_single_compressed_sequences_content: when a frame contains
  a single last compressed block with sequences, output equals executeSequences result

These compose block-loop theorems (decompressBlocksWF_single_compressed_literals_only
and decompressBlocksWF_single_compressed_sequences) through the frame wrapper,
following the same pattern as decompressFrame_single_raw_content and
decompressFrame_single_rle_content.

Closes #973

commit.message:
test: add WebSocket integration tests for all 3 endpoints (#706)

* test: add WebSocket integration tests for UI, session, and legion endpoints

Add 21 integration tests covering all 3 WebSocket endpoints using Starlette
TestClient with MockClaudeSDK fixture replay. Tests verify connection
lifecycle, message broadcasting, multi-client delivery, and keepalive.

New files:
- permission_flow test fixture for permission request/response testing
- test_websocket_ui.py (5 tests): sessions_list, state_change, multi-client, project_updated, ping/pong
- test_websocket_session.py (10 tests): connection, rejection, SDK messages, ordering, permissions, interrupt, multi-client, keepalive
- test_websocket_legion.py (6 tests): connection, rejection, comm broadcast, minion creation, multi-client, ping/pong

Also fixes MockClaudeSDK.send_message() to accept **kwargs for metadata compatibility.

Fixes #229

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* refactor: match MockClaudeSDK.send_message signature to ClaudeSDK

Use explicit `metadata: dict | None = None` parameter instead of
**kwargs to keep the mock's interface aligned with the real SDK.

commit.message:
Fix missing events: split Promise.all into two batches of 5

Supabase silently returns incomplete data when 10+ concurrent queries
run in a single Promise.all. Splitting into two sequential batches
of 5 queries each fixes the issue.

Also removes all debug alerts and cache-busting workarounds.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat(formats): add AIXM, IFC, AIDM parsers and infrastructure

Airport format parsers for industry-standard data:

AIXM Parser (src/formats/aixm/):
- Parse AIXM 5.1.1 XML for runways, taxiways, aprons, navaids
- GML geometry support (Point, LineString, Polygon)
- Coordinate conversion to 3D scene format

IFC Parser (src/formats/ifc/):
- Parse IFC4 BIM files for terminal buildings
- Building storey and space extraction
- Optional ifcopenshell geometry support

AIDM Parser (src/formats/aidm/):
- Parse IATA AIDM 12.0 JSON/XML for operational data
- Flight schedules, resource allocations, events
- Status mapping to internal flight phases

Base infrastructure (src/formats/base.py):
- CoordinateConverter: WGS84 to local 3D scene
- AirportFormatParser abstract base class
- GeoPosition, Position3D data classes

Frontend improvements:
- TrajectoryLine/Trajectory3D alignment fixes
- airport3D.ts and airportLayout.ts constants updates
- FlightContext and map calculation improvements
- vite-env.d.ts TypeScript declarations

Backend improvements:
- Circuit breaker and poll job refinements
- Congestion model updates

Documentation:
- docs/AIRPORT_DATA_IMPORT.md usage guide

Tests: 131 format parser tests + service/route tests

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix number expansion alignment: always split merged IPA chunks

The size() guard (textWords > ipaChunks) missed cases where other
numbers in the same utterance expanded to MORE IPA chunks, masking
the merge. "100 ... 1.95" had 13 text words but 15 IPA chunks, so
splitMultiStressChunks never ran and "one hundred" stayed merged as
wˈʌnhˈʌndɹɪd — shifting every word after it by one position.
"volume" got "colon"'s IPA → "velume".

Fix: always run splitMultiStressChunks when numberRules.enabled.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Reduce information loss: preserve emphasis, hedges, and secondary mentions

Three new rules for --clean prompt:
- Preserve hedges that signal uncertainty (I think, probably, maybe)
- Preserve emphasis/urgency signals (critical, not even close, strong language)
- Keep secondary mentions of tools/platforms even if stated in passing

One new rule for --meta prompt:
- Preserve emphasis and urgency in structured output

Audit of 22 real usage examples showed these three categories were being
over-stripped, losing signal that matters for downstream LLM behavior.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add integer-to-BOOL type conversions (IEC 61131-3 Section 2.5.1.5.1) (#597)

Add support for converting all integer types to BOOL: SINT_TO_BOOL,
INT_TO_BOOL, DINT_TO_BOOL, LINT_TO_BOOL, USINT_TO_BOOL, UINT_TO_BOOL,
UDINT_TO_BOOL, and ULINT_TO_BOOL. These were listed as "Not yet
supported" on the type conversions documentation page.

The conversion uses non-zero → TRUE (1), zero → FALSE (0) semantics,
which requires dedicated CONV_I32_TO_BOOL and CONV_I64_TO_BOOL opcodes
rather than simple bit truncation (which would incorrectly return FALSE
for values like 2 where bit 0 is 0).

https://claude.ai/code/session_01BntRd5og46jAzsrbt2Cbm9

commit.message:
feat: add admin login page to fix Chrome gray screen redirect

The middleware was redirecting to /admin/login which didn't exist, causing
Chrome to show a gray screen. Created proper login page with:

- Clean UI with password authentication
- Bearer token verification via /api/articles test request
- Session cookie storage (admin_session)
- Redirect back to intended admin page after login
- Auto-focus and error handling

Middleware now passes ?redirect= param to preserve intended destination.

Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.ai/code)
via [Happy](https://happy.engineering)

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
Co-Authored-By: Happy <yesreply@happy.engineering>

commit.message:
Add caching and efficiency optimizations to reduce API costs

- URLCache (50MB mem/200MB disk) on APIClient for HTTP-level caching
- Cap assistant chat history to 10 turns to reduce OpenAI token costs
- Cache TTS audio to disk by SHA256 hash to skip ElevenLabs re-fetches
- Replace base64 data URL images with file:// cached on disk
- Deduplicate fridge vision scans with 5-min TTL image hash cache
- Cache ingredient nutrition lookups in memory with 30-min TTL
- Add nutrition date cache (2-min TTL) to skip 5 parallel refetches
- Send lastSyncTimestamp for incremental sync support
- Extract KeychainHelper to its own file

commit.message:
Claude/evaluate app value rs0p b (#1267)

* feat: comprehensive codebase improvements across E2E, agent, observability, merchandise, marketplace, and Python tools

## P0 — Documentation & E2E Test Suite
- Fix CLAUDE.md: correct inflated "60+ spec files" claim to accurate count
- Update playwright.config.ts: add mobile-chrome (Pixel 7) and mobile-safari (iPhone 14) projects, screenshot/video on failure
- Add 9 new E2E spec files: navigation, chat-interaction, agent-flows, creative-persistence, mobile-responsiveness, distribution-workflow, finance-workflow, maestro-workflows, chaos
- Add e2e/fixtures/auth.ts: Firebase auth bypass fixture for CI

## P1 — Agent Module UI Integration
- AgentDashboard.tsx: add chat tab wired to agentService + ChatMessage + PromptArea; add tasks tab; auto-scroll on new messages
- AgentSidebar.tsx: extend tab union type to include 'chat' | 'tasks'; refactor to data-driven NAV_ITEMS array
- SpecialistSelector.tsx: new component pulling real agent list from agentRegistry.getAll(), dropdown with auto-routing option
- TaskTracker.tsx: new component displaying live Maestro batchingTasks from root agentSlice with status badges and priority indicators

## P1 — Observability Dashboard
- ObservabilityDashboard.tsx: expand from 10-line stub to full tabbed layout (Traces / Metrics / Health / Circuit Breaker)
- MetricsDashboard.tsx: calls MetricsService.getSystemMetrics(), time range selector (1d/7d/30d), Recharts agent breakdown bar chart
- HealthPanel.tsx: real health checks for Firestore, Agent Zero sidecar, Gemini API key; auto-refresh every 30s
- CircuitBreakerPanel.tsx: reads MembershipService.checkBudget(), displays breaker state (closed/half-open/open)

## P2 — Merchandise Real Integration
- PODCredentialService.ts: new service for Firestore-backed API key storage and live key validation per provider
- PODIntegrationPanel.tsx: replace fake setTimeout mocks with real API key modal + PODCredentialService + PrintOnDemandService.getProducts()
- pod_integration_tool.py: replace pure mock with real httpx calls to Printful and Printify REST APIs

## P2 — Marketplace Module
- constants.ts: add 'marketplace' to MODULE_IDS
- App.tsx: register MarketplaceStorefront in MODULE_COMPONENTS lazy-load map
- marketplaceSlice.ts: new Zustand slice with cart state (CartItem[], addToCart, removeFromCart, clearCart, cartTotal)
- store/index.ts: wire marketplaceSlice into root store

## P2 — Python Tools Determinism
- isrc_upc_auto_assigner.py: replace AI-driven generation with deterministic ISRC (CC-XXX-YY-NNNNN format, sequential counter) and UPC (EAN-13 check digit algorithm)
- waterfall_calculator.py: add input validation (rate bounds, currency normalization, structured error responses)
- recoupment_calculator.py: add same input validation patterns

https://claude.ai/code/session_01UbbhtT9KxgV9y9WNofau98

* feat: make mechanical royalties projection and metadata QC auditor deterministic

- mechanical_royalties_projection.py: add input validation (rate bounds 0-100%, currency normalization, negative value checks, structured error responses)
- metadata_qc_auditor.py: replace AI-driven title checks with full deterministic rule-based QC engine covering ISRC format (regex), UPC digit validation, field length limits, ISO 8601 date format, DSP-accepted genre list, required field presence — outputs structured JSON with per-field pass/fail/warn and aggregate score

https://claude.ai/code/session_01UbbhtT9KxgV9y9WNofau98

* fix: add input validation and remove broken AI code from recoupment_calculator.py

Replace unparseable AI-call block with deterministic calculation and proper
input validation: advance_amount >= 0, royalty_percentage in [0,100],
avg_per_stream_rate > 0, currency normalization via _normalize_currency().

https://claude.ai/code/session_01UbbhtT9KxgV9y9WNofau98

* docs: add session handoff document for IDE resumption

https://claude.ai/code/session_01UbbhtT9KxgV9y9WNofau98

* fix: repair malformed import in FinanceDashboard.tsx

Duplicate `import {` token on line 16 was colliding with the
ModuleErrorBoundary import, causing TS1003/TS1005/TS1128 parse errors.
Moved ModuleErrorBoundary import before the lucide-react block.

https://claude.ai/code/session_01UbbhtT9KxgV9y9WNofau98

commit.message:
Implement intelligent deficit detection to filter idle-throttled false positives

## Problem
System was alerting on multi-day solar deficits caused by MPPT throttling (batteries full, Float/Storage state), not actual energy problems. This created false positives wasting field tech time.

## Solution: Context-Aware Deficit Detection
Distinguish "real" vs "idle-throttled" deficits using end-of-day state:
- **Thresholds:** SOC ≥88%, MPPT Float (5) or Storage (6), deficit <1 kWh
- **Approach:** Idle-throttled day breaks deficit streak (conservative)
- **Transparency:** Show throttled status throughout UI, emails, semantic queries

## Changes

### Backend (server/server.js, db.js)
- Add extractMpptState() helper to normalize MPPT charge state from snapshots
- Add isRealDeficit() classifier with user-specified thresholds
- Update computeAlerts() to only count real deficit days (throttled breaks streak)
- Track end-of-day SOC and MPPT state in dailyEnergy Map
- Add battery_soc_eod, mppt_state_eod columns to daily_energy_summary table
- Update upsertDailyEnergy() to persist EOD state
- Update FLEET_SCHEMA with deficit context for semantic queries

### Frontend (EnergyPage.jsx, FleetOverview.jsx, index.css)
- Show 🔋 Throttled badge on deficit days in energy alerts
- Add muted background for throttled deficit rows
- Annotate action queue items with "(includes throttled days)"
- Add CSS styles: .deficit-row-throttled, .throttle-indicator, .action-throttle-note

### Email Notifications (email.js)
- Add orange THROTTLED badge to deficit table rows
- Add footer note explaining throttling logic
- Update digest filtering to track has_throttled transparency field

### Semantic Search (embeddings.js)
- Enrich alertToText() with throttle context
- Example: "Alert for Trailer 15. 3 consecutive days of real energy deficit. (1 throttled days excluded). Severity: warning."

## Expected Impact
- Reduce false positive alerts by ~40-60%
- Field techs can trust alerts indicate real problems
- Consistency with existing solar score throttling logic
- Full transparency: throttled days visible, just not flagged critical

## Technical Details
- Database migration adds 2 columns (battery_soc_eod REAL, mppt_state_eod INTEGER)
- Backward compatible: existing data remains valid, new fields populate over time
- MPPT states: 0=Off, 3=Bulk, 4=Absorption, 5=Float, 6=Storage, 7=Equalize
- Formula: throttled = (SOC ≥88% AND MPPT=Float/Storage AND deficit <1kWh)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: add Image Strength slider for I2V motion control

With image_strength=1.0 (previous default), the first frame was fully
locked to the source image and temporal attention kept subsequent frames
too close, resulting in minimal motion (Ken Burns zoom effect).

Changes:
- Default image_strength lowered to 0.85 for more motion freedom
- New "Image Strength" slider (50-100%) in the UI when image is loaded
- image_strength passed through the full pipeline: UI → API → pipeline → MLX
- Lower values = more motion/prompt influence, higher = closer to source

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Team Builder: T3 must differ from T1 by ≥2 starters

Added retry loop for T3: if T3 shares >4 starters with T1 (Best Team),
exclude the weakest shared starter and retry findBestTeam (up to 6 retries).
Guarantees Alternative B always offers ≥2 genuinely different starters
vs Best Team, while still maximizing FPT within that constraint.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

security: encrypt BYOK API keys, enforce webhook verification, add proxy budget checks

- Add Fernet encryption for user-provided API keys (BYOK) — keys were stored
  as plaintext in the encrypted_key column. Requires ENCRYPTION_KEY env var.
- Restrict Clerk webhook verification bypass to dev/test/local environments
  only — production and staging now reject webhooks if CLERK_WEBHOOK_SECRET
  is not configured (HTTP 500).
- Add budget enforcement to proxy endpoints — containers can no longer make
  unlimited API calls when a user exceeds their plan budget.
- Fix broken _send_channel_rpc (was passing wrong args to pool.send_rpc).
- Fix billing._get_billing_account calling removed get_billing_account_for_org.
- Fix get_optional_user silently dropping org claims from JWT.
- Remove dead org code (require_org_context, require_org_admin,
  create_customer_for_org, get_billing_account_for_org).
- Remove unused FREE_TIER_LIMIT alias.
- Refactor debug endpoints with shared require_non_production dependency.
- Convert settings_keys router to use get_db dependency injection.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix live scores: goal scorers loading, penalty display, and FT status lag

Bug 1: fixtures-controller required league on every request so fetching
by id returned 400 - silently swallowed in league.js leaving LOADING
forever, and crashing events.js entirely. Accept id without league;
service forwards id with a separate cache key (by-ID responses include
events array, by-league do not).

Bug 2: fixture.goals holds 90-min score only. Penalty result is in
fixture.score.penalty. Both league.js and events.js now append (A-Bp)
to the score when penalty values are present.

Bug 3: Replaced fixed 5-min setInterval with adaptive polling - 60s
when any fixture is live, 5min when all matches are done.
